mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower rates compress required returns on stocks, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer spending. This dual demand shock predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the joint demand signal and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 0.5% month-over-month signals expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength. This supply expansion supports profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when INDPRO growth > 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100,000 jobs per month combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.5% year-over-year) signals stagflationary pressures. The Fed will maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100,000 AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint stagflation signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings. This demand-side strength produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as profit expectations rise.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during above-2.5% GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) declining month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0% signals the Fed is in restrictive hold mode. This combination of disinflation and high rates creates uncertainty about policy duration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerating above 1,300 thousand units annualized signals strong residential investment and forward-looking demand. This housing strength predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as construction and related sectors benefit.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no correlation with HOUST levels (Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window), or positive HOUST readings fail to predict positive returns in > 50% of instances.
